data.content:
### Display Multivariate Data with Radar Chart in Recharts

Source: https://context7.com/recharts/recharts/llms.txt

Visualize and compare multiple variables across categories using a radar/spider chart format. Displays multivariate data for two entities (Student A and Student B) with filled areas, custom colors, and configurable opacity. Uses PolarGrid, PolarAngleAxis, and PolarRadiusAxis for proper polar coordinate system setup.

```jsx
import React from 'react';
import { RadarChart, PolarGrid, PolarAngleAxis, PolarRadiusAxis, Radar, Legend, ResponsiveContainer } from 'recharts';

const data = [
  { subject: 'Math', A: 120, B: 110, fullMark: 150 },
  { subject: 'Chinese', A: 98, B: 130, fullMark: 150 },
  { subject: 'English', A: 86, B: 130, fullMark: 150 },
  { subject: 'Geography', A: 99, B: 100, fullMark: 150 },
  { subject: 'Physics', A: 85, B: 90, fullMark: 150 },
  { subject: 'History', A: 65, B: 85, fullMark: 150 }
];

function RadarChartExample() {
  return (
    <ResponsiveContainer width="100%" height={400}>
      <RadarChart cx="50%" cy="50%" outerRadius="80%" data={data}>
        <PolarGrid />
        <PolarAngleAxis dataKey="subject" />
        <PolarRadiusAxis angle={90} domain={[0, 150]} />
        <Radar
          name="Student A"
          dataKey="A"
          stroke="#8884d8"
          fill="#8884d8"
          fillOpacity={0.6}
        />
        <Radar
          name="Student B"
          dataKey="B"
          stroke="#82ca9d"
          fill="#82ca9d"
          fillOpacity={0.6}
        />
        <Legend />
      </RadarChart>
    </ResponsiveContainer>
  );
}

export default RadarChartExample;
```

--------------------------------

### Render Recharts Polygon Component (JSX)

Source: https://github.com/recharts/recharts/blob/main/storybook/stories/API/shapes/Polygon.mdx

Demonstrates how to render the `Polygon` component in Recharts, passing its properties as JSX attributes. This component is a fundamental building block for other Recharts elements like `Radar` charts.

```jsx
<Polygon {...polygonProps} />
```

--------------------------------

### Display Component Properties with ArgTypes

Source: https://github.com/recharts/recharts/blob/main/storybook/stories/API/chart/RadarChart.mdx

Renders an interactive ArgTypes table displaying all configurable properties of the RadarChart component, sorted with required properties first. This allows developers to explore and understand the component's API directly in the documentation.

```JavaScript
<ArgTypes of={RadarChartStories} sort={'requiredFirst'} />
```

--------------------------------

### Render Interactive Canvas with Simple RadarChart Story

Source: https://github.com/recharts/recharts/blob/main/storybook/stories/API/chart/RadarChart.mdx

Displays an interactive Canvas component showing the Simple RadarChart story with padded layout. This provides a visual example of the RadarChart component in action within the Storybook documentation.

```JavaScript
<Canvas of={RadarChartStories.Simple} layout='padded'/>
```

--------------------------------

### Define Solar System Data Array for Recharts

Source: https://github.com/recharts/recharts/wiki/Axis-domains-and-ticks

This JavaScript array defines the data points for celestial bodies in a solar system, including properties like name, orbital distance, radius, mass, and a fill color. It serves as input for Recharts components to visualize planetary data, highlighting objects with significantly different values.

```jsx
const solarSystem = [
  {
    name: 'Sun',
    orbitalDistanceKm: 0,
    radiusKm: 696340,
    massKg: 1.989e30,
    fill: '#ffa700',
  },
  {
    name: 'Mercury',
    orbitalDistanceKm: 5.791e7,
    radiusKm: 2439.7,
    massKg: 3.3e23,
    fill: '#1a1a1a',
  },
  // ... rest of the planets
]
```

--------------------------------

### Define Storybook Meta Configuration for RadarChart

Source: https://github.com/recharts/recharts/blob/main/storybook/stories/API/chart/RadarChart.mdx

Configures the Storybook meta information for the RadarChart component using the imported RadarChartStories module. This establishes the component context and metadata for the documentation page.

```JavaScript
<Meta of={RadarChartStories} />
```

--------------------------------

### Brush Component - Interactive Data Filtering and Zooming

Source: https://context7.com/recharts/recharts/llms.txt

Implements an interactive brush selector to filter and zoom through large datasets in a line chart. The Brush component displays a preview of data with adjustable range handles (startIndex and endIndex). It accepts an onChange callback to track range modifications and works with ResponsiveContainer for responsive layouts.

```jsx
import React from 'react';
import { LineChart, Line, Brush, XAxis, YAxis, CartesianGrid, Tooltip, ResponsiveContainer } from 'recharts';

const generateData = () => {
  const data = [];
  for (let i = 0; i < 100; i++) {
    data.push({
      name: `Point ${i}`,
      value: Math.random() * 1000 + 100,
    });
  }
  return data;
};

const data = generateData();

function BrushExample() {
  return (
    <ResponsiveContainer width="100%" height={400}>
      <LineChart
        data={data}
        margin={{ top: 5, right: 30, left: 20, bottom: 5 }}
      >
        <CartesianGrid strokeDasharray="3 3" />
        <XAxis dataKey="name" />
        <YAxis />
        <Tooltip />
        <Line type="monotone" dataKey="value" stroke="#8884d8" dot={false} />

        {/* Brush for data filtering */}
        <Brush
          dataKey="name"
          height={30}
          stroke="#8884d8"
          startIndex={0}
          endIndex={20}
          onChange={(range) => console.log('Brush range:', range)}
        >
          <LineChart>
            <Line type="monotone" dataKey="value" stroke="#8884d8" dot={false} />
          </LineChart>
        </Brush>
      </LineChart>
    </ResponsiveContainer>
  );
}

export default BrushExample;
```

--------------------------------

### Add CartesianGrid, XAxis, and YAxis to Recharts LineChart (TypeScript/JSX)

Source: https://github.com/recharts/recharts/blob/main/storybook/stories/GettingStarted.mdx

This code expands on the basic line chart by incorporating essential visual components like `CartesianGrid`, `XAxis`, and `YAxis`. It imports the necessary components and configures the X-axis to display data based on the 'name' key, enhancing readability and data interpretation.

```tsx
import { LineChart, Line, CartesianGrid, XAxis, YAxis } from 'recharts';
const renderLineChart = (
  <LineChart width={600} height={300} data={data}>
    <Line type="monotone" dataKey="uv" stroke="#8884d8" />
    <CartesianGrid stroke="#ccc" />
    <XAxis dataKey="name" />
    <YAxis />
  </LineChart>
);
```

```tsx
<LineChart width={600} height={300} data={data}>
  <Line type="monotone" dataKey="uv" stroke="#8884d8" />
  <CartesianGrid stroke="#ccc" />
  <XAxis dataKey="name" />
  <YAxis />
</LineChart>
```

--------------------------------

### Import Storybook Documentation Components and RadarChart Stories

Source: https://github.com/recharts/recharts/blob/main/storybook/stories/API/chart/RadarChart.mdx

Imports Storybook addon-docs blocks (Canvas, Meta, ArgTypes) for documentation rendering and the RadarChart stories module. These imports enable interactive documentation generation and story composition in Storybook.

```JavaScript
import { Canvas, Meta, ArgTypes } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks';
import * as RadarChartStories from './RadarChart.stories';
```

--------------------------------

### Create a RadialBarChart with Recharts in React

Source: https://context7.com/recharts/recharts/llms.txt

This snippet demonstrates how to render a RadialBarChart component using Recharts in a React application. It configures the chart with sample data, sets properties like inner and outer radii, and includes essential sub-components such as RadialBar, Legend, and Tooltip for comprehensive data representation. The chart is wrapped in a ResponsiveContainer to ensure it adapts to its parent's dimensions.

```jsx
import React from 'react';
import { RadialBarChart, RadialBar, Legend, Tooltip, ResponsiveContainer } from 'recharts';

const data = [
  { name: '18-24', uv: 31.47, fill: '#8884d8' },
  { name: '25-29', uv: 26.69, fill: '#83a6ed' },
  { name: '30-34', uv: 15.69, fill: '#8dd1e1' },
  { name: '35-39', uv: 8.22, fill: '#82ca9d' },
  { name: '40-49', uv: 8.63, fill: '#a4de6c' },
  { name: '50+', uv: 2.63, fill: '#d0ed57' },
  { name: 'Unknown', uv: 6.67, fill: '#ffc658' },
];

function RadialBarChartExample() {
  return (
    <ResponsiveContainer width="100%" height={400}>
      <RadialBarChart
        cx="50%"
        cy="50%"
        innerRadius="10%"
        outerRadius="80%"
        barSize={10}
        data={data}
      >
        <RadialBar
          minAngle={15}
          label={{ position: 'insideStart', fill: '#fff' }}
          background
          clockWise
          dataKey="uv"
        />
        <Legend
          iconSize={10}
          layout="vertical"
          verticalAlign="middle"
          align="right"
        />
        <Tooltip />
      </RadialBarChart>
    </ResponsiveContainer>
  );
}

export default RadialBarChartExample;
```

--------------------------------

### Apply Custom Domain with Data Overflow for Recharts YAxis

Source: https://github.com/recharts/recharts/wiki/Axis-domains-and-ticks

This Recharts YAxis configuration defines a custom numerical domain using `domain={[1e23, 2e27]}`. By setting `allowDataOverflow` to `true`, the axis will strictly adhere to the specified domain, effectively cutting off data points that fall outside this range, such as the Sun's mass in the solar system example.

```jsx
<YAxis
  domain={[1e23, 2e27]}
  allowDataOverflow
/>
```

--------------------------------

### Build Scatter Chart with Multi-dimensional Data in Recharts

Source: https://context7.com/recharts/recharts/llms.txt

Create a scatter plot visualization with multiple data series, Z-axis scaling for bubble sizes, and custom cell colors. Supports multiple scatter groups with different shapes and styling options. Uses CartesianGrid, Tooltip, and Legend components for enhanced interactivity and data clarity.

```jsx
import React from 'react';
import { ScatterChart, Scatter, XAxis, YAxis, ZAxis, CartesianGrid, Tooltip, Legend, ResponsiveContainer, Cell } from 'recharts';

const data01 = [
  { x: 100, y: 200, z: 200 },
  { x: 120, y: 100, z: 260 },
  { x: 170, y: 300, z: 400 },
  { x: 140, y: 250, z: 280 },
  { x: 150, y: 400, z: 500 },
  { x: 110, y: 280, z: 200 }
];

const data02 = [
  { x: 300, y: 300, z: 200 },
  { x: 400, y: 500, z: 260 },
  { x: 200, y: 700, z: 400 },
  { x: 340, y: 350, z: 280 },
  { x: 560, y: 500, z: 500 }
];

const COLORS = ['#0088FE', '#00C49F', '#FFBB28', '#FF8042', '#8884d8'];

function ScatterChartExample() {
  return (
    <ResponsiveContainer width="100%" height={400}>
      <ScatterChart margin={{ top: 20, right: 20, bottom: 20, left: 20 }}>
        <CartesianGrid />
        <XAxis type="number" dataKey="x" name="weight" unit="kg" />
        <YAxis type="number" dataKey="y" name="height" unit="cm" />
        <ZAxis type="number" dataKey="z" range={[60, 400]} name="score" unit="points" />
        <Tooltip cursor={{ strokeDasharray: '3 3' }} />
        <Legend />
        <Scatter name="Group A" data={data01} fill="#8884d8">
          {data01.map((entry, index) => (
            <Cell key={`cell-${index}`} fill={COLORS[index % COLORS.length]} />
          ))}
        </Scatter>
        <Scatter name="Group B" data={data02} fill="#82ca9d" shape="triangle" />
      </ScatterChart>
    </ResponsiveContainer>
  );
}

export default ScatterChartExample;
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Recharts YAxis as Category Type

Source: https://github.com/recharts/recharts/wiki/Axis-domains-and-ticks

This Recharts YAxis configuration sets the `type` prop to 'category'. This treats numerical data as discrete categorical values, displaying each unique mass value as a separate tick rather than a continuous spectrum, which can lead to unsorted and unrounded ticks.

```jsx
<YAxis type='category' label={{ value: 'Mass [kg]', position: 'insideLeft', dx: 0, dy: 20, angle: -90 }} />
```
